methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

path: Multicall to InitCore: borrow -> callback -> mintTo -> collateralize
coreCallback sends ALL tokenIn balance to swapHelper
collToken sent to collPool, minted, collateralized to position
data_flows.trust_changes: User input transitions from user-controlled to protocol-managed via multicall
validation_points: tokenIn must be collToken or borrToken
exposure_risks: coreCallback uses balanceOf(this) for amtIn - residual tokens from prior operations get included
SwapHelper gets full token balance, could extract value if malicious
manipulation_risks: Swap routing controlled by user-provided _data parameter
MEV sandwich attacks on the swap

path: Order loaded from storage, marginPos loaded
_calculateRepaySize returns (repayAmt, repayShares) - SWAPPED at call site
Executor transfers borrToken (repayAmt=shares_value) and tokenOut (amtOut) to hook/recipient
transformations: Debt shares -> token amounts via debtShareToAmtCurrent
data_flows.trust_changes: Order parameters trusted from storage but can be manipulated via updateOrder missing auth
validation_points: Trigger price validated against oracle markPrice
Limit price used for amtOut calculation
exposure_risks: Return value swap in _calculateRepaySize causes incorrect amounts
updateOrder has no authorization check allowing anyone to modify order parameters
manipulation_risks: Attacker updates victim's order via updateOrder (no auth check)
Sets tokenOut to worthless token, extreme triggerPrice/limitPrice
Fills the modified order to steal collateral

path: Liquidator calls liquidate with posId, repay pool, collateral pool
_repay: transfers borrToken from liquidator to pool, reduces debt
Liquidation incentive calculated based on health factor
Collateral shares calculated using oracle prices and incentive
Collateral transferred from position to liquidator
Post-liquidation health check (bypassed if health was 0)
data_flows.trust_changes: Oracle prices trusted for incentive and collateral calculation
exposure_risks: Oracle manipulation could affect liquidation incentive and collateral calculation
health_e18 == 0 returns multiplier 0 from LiqIncentiveCalculator - no incentive for underwater positions
manipulation_risks: Price manipulation to trigger unnecessary liquidations
minLiqIncentiveMultiplier can exceed maxLiqIncentiveMultiplier via misconfiguration

path: Get LP token reserves (r0, r1) and prices from oracle
Price used for collateral valuation in InitCore
transformations: Reserves + oracle prices -> LP token price
Square root calculations for fair pricing
sinks: LP price used in collateral credit calculation
LP price used in liquidation calculations
data_flows.trust_changes: On-chain reserves trusted directly when kLast == 0
exposure_risks: When kLast == 0, reserves can be manipulated via flash loan + swap
Inflated LP price leads to excessive borrowing against LP collateral
manipulation_risks: Flash loan token0 -> swap in pair -> inflate r1/deflate r0 -> LP price increases
Reverse swap and repay flash loan

path: Each borrow/decollateralize adds posId to uncheckedPosIds instead of immediate health check
After all calls, iterate uncheckedPosIds and verify health >= 1e18
transformations: Individual operations deferred health check to batch end
sinks: Position state changes committed if all health checks pass
data_flows.trust_changes: Temporary unhealthy states allowed during multicall batch
validation_points: Health check at end of multicall for all affected positions
exposure_risks: callback function allows external calls during multicall - potential for complex attack sequences
Callback can trigger arbitrary code execution on external contracts
manipulation_risks: Complex multi-step attacks that leave positions healthy at end but extract value during execution

path: User provides msg.value and repay params
If WNATIVE: wraps msg.value, reduces repayAmt by msg.value
transformations: Debt shares -> token amount via debtShareToAmtCurrent
data_flows.trust_changes: User-provided shares validated against position debt
validation_points: repayShares capped at position debt shares
Repay amount calculated after interest accrual
exposure_risks: msg.value consumed by first WNATIVE repay, but msg.value immutable in call context
Second WNATIVE repay would try to re-deposit same msg.value, failing

core_rules: Positions must maintain health >= 1e18 (collateral credit >= borrow credit) after state-changing operations
Collateral credit = sum(tokenValue * collFactor) with collFactor <= 1e18
Borrow credit = sum(tokenValue * borrFactor) with borrFactor >= 1e18, rounded up
Debt ceiling per mode per pool enforced by RiskManager on borrow
Virtual shares (10^8) and virtual assets (1) used to mitigate inflation attacks
Interest accrues per second based on double-slope IRM
Reserve factor portion of interest minted to treasury
Liquidation only when health < 1e18, post-liquidation health capped at maxHealthAfterLiq
economic_incentives: Lenders earn interest proportional to utilization and time
Borrowers pay interest that increases with utilization (double-slope)
Liquidators receive collateral worth (repayAmt * liqIncentiveMultiplier) - incentivized to maintain system health
Margin order executors profit from the spread between oracle price and execution terms
Protocol treasury accumulates reserve factor portion of all interest
governance_mechanisms: Governor can set oracle sources, collateral/borrow factors, mode configurations, quote assets
Guardian can set pool configs, IRM, reserve factor, risk manager parameters, max collateral counts
No timelock observed on governance actions - immediate effect

edge_cases.scenario: updateOrder called by non-owner to modify another user's active order parameters
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Only the order owner (position owner) should be able to modify their orders
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Any user with any valid position can modify any active order's triggerPrice, limitPrice, collAmt, and tokenOut. The function checks initPosId != 0 for the caller's position but never verifies order.initPosId == initPosId
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Attacker modifies victim's order: sets tokenOut to worthless ERC20, sets extreme triggerPrice to ensure fillability, sets limitPrice to minimize amtOut. Then fills the order as executor: pays borrToken for debt repayment, sends near-zero worthless tokens as amtOut to victim, receives victim's real collateral. Profit = collateral value - debt repaid.

edge_cases.scenario: _calculateRepaySize return values destructured in wrong order in _calculateFillOrderInfo
edge_cases.expected_behavior: repayShares should receive share count, repayAmt should receive token amount
edge_cases.actual_behavior: _calculateRepaySize returns (repayAmt, repayShares) but call site destructures as (repayShares, repayAmt) = _calculateRepaySize(...), swapping the values. repayShares gets the token amount and repayAmt gets the share count.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: In fillOrder: executor transfers too few borrTokens (share count instead of token amount), tries to repay too many shares (token amount instead of share count). Transaction likely reverts for non-1:1 share ratios, but for early pools with 1:1 ratio, incorrect economic outcomes occur. All order fills are affected.

edge_cases.scenario: fillOrder auto-cancel when position collateral is 0 only updates memory, not storage
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Order status should be set to Cancelled in storage when position has no collateral
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Order is loaded into memory (Order memory order = __orders[_orderId]), memory copy's status is set to Cancelled, but __orders[_orderId] in storage remains Active. The CancelOrder event is emitted but the order remains fillable.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Order remains permanently Active in storage. If collateral is later re-added to the position, the order could be filled. Also causes repeated gas-wasting calls and misleading events.

edge_cases.scenario: LP price calculation using reserves when kLast == 0
edge_cases.actual_behavior: When kLast == 0, price = 2 * sqrt(r0*p0) * sqrt(r1*p1) / totalSupply uses live reserves that can be manipulated via flash-loan-funded swaps
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Flash loan attack: borrow large amount of token0, swap into pair to skew reserves, LP price inflates, borrow against wrapped LP collateral, reverse swap, repay flash loan, keep borrowed assets. Net extraction limited by collateral factor but could be significant.

edge_cases.scenario: LiqIncentiveCalculator returns 0 when health factor is exactly 0
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Maximum incentive should be offered for deeply underwater positions
edge_cases.actual_behavior: When _healthFactor_e18 == 0, getLiqIncentiveMultiplier_e18 returns 0. In liquidate, repayAmtWithLiqIncentive = repayAmt * 0 / 1e18 = 0, so liquidator gets 0 collateral shares.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Positions with exactly 0 health (zero collateral credit but nonzero debt) cannot be profitably liquidated, leading to bad debt accumulation. In practice, health reaching exactly 0 is rare but possible with collFactor set to 0 for a token.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should validate position exists before operating
edge_cases.actual_behavior: initPosId defaults to 0 for non-existent positions. _reducePosInternal reads __marginPositions[0] which has zero addresses. Subsequent pool calls on address(0) would revert, but the error is unclear.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: No direct value extraction but inconsistent error handling compared to other functions that check initPosId != 0

edge_cases.scenario: Multiple orders reference same position collateral without aggregate check
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Total collateral committed across all orders should not exceed position collateral
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Each order's collAmt is checked individually against current position collateral at creation time. Multiple orders can be created that collectively exceed total collateral.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: When orders are filled sequentially, later orders find insufficient collateral. _calculateRepaySize caps order.collAmt at remaining collateral, but proportional repay is lower than expected. Order owner may receive less value than anticipated.

edge_cases.scenario: coreCallback transfers entire token balance to swapHelper
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Only the specific operation's tokens should be swapped
edge_cases.actual_behavior: uint amtIn = IERC20(swapInfo.tokenIn).balanceOf(address(this)); transfers ALL balance. If prior operations left residual tokens, they are included in the swap.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If tokens are accidentally sent to the hook contract, they will be swept into the next user's operation. Could be exploited by donating tokens to manipulate swap amounts, though net benefit to the next user rather than the attacker.

edge_cases.scenario: MoneyMarketHook msg.value used in multiple WNATIVE operations
edge_cases.expected_behavior: msg.value should be usable across operations or properly tracked
edge_cases.actual_behavior: msg.value is immutable within a call. First WNATIVE deposit consumes all ETH. Second WNATIVE deposit call attempts to send msg.value again but has insufficient balance, causing revert.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: No security issue but users submitting multiple WNATIVE repay/deposit params will get unexpected reverts. Documented as 'msg.value should be used for 1 operation only' but not enforced.

edge_cases.scenario: InitOracle with two valid sources but maxPriceDeviation not set
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should function with default deviation or skip check
edge_cases.actual_behavior: maxPriceDeviations_e18[_token] defaults to 0. Deviation check: (maxPrice * 1e18) / minPrice <= 0 always fails since prices > 0. Oracle reverts for any token with two valid sources but no deviation set.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Configuration oversight blocks oracle functionality. No direct exploit but could cause denial of service for affected tokens.

edge_cases.actual_behavior: No validation that min <= max. Math.max(Math.min(value, max), min) means min wins when min > max, potentially giving excessive liquidation incentives.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Governor misconfiguration leads to excessive liquidation incentives, potentially draining positions during liquidation.

edge_cases.scenario: First wrap to WLpMoeMasterChef for a pid with lpSupply == 0
edge_cases.actual_behavior: updateRewards modifier: if lpSupply == 0, reward per share is not incremented. Any rewards distributed by MasterChef during the deposit call are lost since they cannot be attributed to any LP holder.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Small amount of rewards lost on first deposit to a pid. Not exploitable for profit but represents value leakage.

assumption_violations: Assumption: Order parameters can only be modified by position owner. Violation: updateOrder has no authorization check linking order to caller's position.
Assumption: _calculateRepaySize returns are correctly matched at call site. Violation: Return destructuring is swapped in _calculateFillOrderInfo.
Assumption: LP reserves accurately reflect fair value. Violation: Reserves can be manipulated when kLast == 0.
Assumption: Liquidation incentive is always positive for unhealthy positions. Violation: Returns 0 when health == 0.
Assumption: Storage is updated when fillOrder auto-cancels. Violation: Memory-only update.
game_theory_risks: Order executors can front-run each other to capture fill opportunities (MEV)
Liquidators can manipulate oracle prices (if possible) to trigger liquidations and capture incentives
Users can create orders with extreme parameters to bait executors into unprofitable fills
With updateOrder vulnerability: attacker can systematically steal collateral from all users with active orders
Flash loan-based LP price manipulation to borrow against inflated collateral, then default
integration_risks: Merchant Moe MasterChef reward distribution assumes standard ERC20 reward tokens
MasterChef extraRewarder returning native token (address(0)) handled via WNATIVE wrapping in receive()
SwapHelper is trusted to perform honest swaps - malicious helper could steal tokens
Oracle failure of both primary and secondary sources causes complete system halt for affected tokens
Moe pair kLast depends on fee-on state which is controlled by Moe protocol governance
calculation_errors: CRITICAL: _calculateFillOrderInfo destructures _calculateRepaySize return as (repayShares, repayAmt) but function returns (repayAmt, repayShares) - values are swapped
In calculatePrice_e36 with kLast != 0: intermediate division prices_e36[0].mulDiv(prices_e36[1], totalSupply) may lose precision for tokens with very different decimal scales
getBorrowCreditCurrent_e36 uses ceilDiv for final division favoring borrowers (increasing borrow credit), which is correct for safety but reduces capital efficiency

token_semantics.notes: Wrapped native token (WETH/WMANTLE). Used for native ETH handling. Deposit/withdraw pattern.
Merchant Moe reward token distributed via MasterChef
Dynamic decimals = underlyingToken.decimals() + 8 (VIRTUAL_SHARE_DECIMALS). Only mintable/burnable by InitCore.
Rebasing token (MUSD wrapping USDY). Uses shares-based accounting internally. Handled via mUSDUSDYHelper.
Yield-bearing non-rebasing token. Used as underlying in lending pools. mUSD wraps/unwraps to USDY.
Merchant Moe AMM LP tokens. Wrapped into WLpMoeMasterChef NFTs for collateral use. kLast may be 0 if fee switch is off.

invariants.name: position_health
invariants.rule: After any non-liquidation state change, collateralCredit_e36 / borrowCredit_e36 >= 1e18 (health >= 1)

invariants.name: debt_share_consistency
invariants.rule: sum of all position debt shares for a pool should equal pool.totalDebtShares

invariants.name: collateral_balance_tracking
invariants.rule: __collBalances[pool] == IERC20(pool).balanceOf(POS_MANAGER) and sum of all position collateral for pool <= __collBalances[pool]

invariants.name: reward_accounting
invariants.rule: pidAccRewardPerShares_e18 >= idAccRewardPerShares_e18 for all active wrapped LP tokens
